_units: Mixed: node coordinates in m, E in MPa (N/mm²), body-force rho in kg/m³ and g in m/s². This combination is internally consistent: strains (B·u) are dimensionless, stresses (E·ε) come out in MPa, and displacements are in mm (NOT m). For a fully consistent N-mm-MPa system use node coordinates in mm, rho in t/mm³ (e.g. 2.4e-9), g in mm/s² (e.g. -9810).

loads._comment: Gravity in -Z. rho=2400 kg/m³ (concrete). Unit cube volume=1 m³, weight≈23544 N.

_comment: Unit concrete cube fixed at the bottom face (z=0), self-weight under gravity.
